goal: Create a weekly schedule including three workouts per week: two mornings and one flexible (morning-or-evening on home day); maintain 5:30 PM pickup and 8:00 AM on-site start; include family time.
constraints: Team expects people to be logged in on-site by 8:00 AM.
Schedule workouts around 5:30 PM pickup and 8:00 AM start.
Be asleep by ~11:00 PM most nights.
There are two weekly evening commitments that I can't miss — they take place on different weeknights.
Take medication twice daily with ~12-hour spacing; exact times are flexible relative to meals.
decisions: Keep morning workouts as default; allow one workout to be moved to evening on home days.
entities: Attach workouts to an existing morning anchor (after breakfast) to form a habit; plan 20–30 minute sessions; three sessions per week.
open: Begin step-by-step: collect Work schedule details (days, start/end times, remote/hybrid/on-site).
Clarify target sleep schedule: bedtime, wake time, or total hours of sleep.
Confirm partner availability schedule for mornings (which days).
Finalize specific workout days/times for the week (two fixed morning slots and one flexible on home day).
Decide workout duration (e.g., 20–30 minutes) and type (bodyweight/cardio) and how it fits into morning routine.
Decide which two days to work from home (or should I pick logical ones).
Decide workout duration and type options for the week (e.g., 20–30 min bodyweight/cardio; 45–60 min gym/lift; or walks).
Identify two weeknights with non-missable commitments and plan workouts around avoiding those nights.
Account for weeks when partner is traveling and not home by 6 PM: determine fallback workouts or scheduling changes.
Determine medication timing windows (two daily doses, ~12 hours apart) and how they align with meals and workouts.
